task_completion.description: Verify that the sales_data.csv file has been imported into Power BI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Data' or 'Fields' pane on the right side of the Power BI Desktop interface. You should see a table (likely named 'sales_data') expanded to show columns such as 'Region', 'Sales_Amount', and 'Quantity_Sold'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the three required base measures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Data' or 'Fields' pane, look for items with a calculator icon named 'Total_Revenue', 'Total_Units', and 'Total_Transactions'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the disconnected parameter table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Data' or 'Fields' pane, look for a distinct table named 'Metric_Param' that contains a column named 'Metric'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the dynamic selection and title measures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Data' or 'Fields' pane, look for items with a calculator icon named 'Selected_Metric_Value' and 'Chart_Title'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a slicer visual is present on the canvas for metric selection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main report canvas for a slicer visual. It should display selectable options corresponding to the metrics: 'Revenue', 'Units', and 'Transactions'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the clustered bar chart is configured with regions and a dynamic title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main report canvas for a clustered bar chart. The categorical axis should display regions (e.g., North, South, East, West). The title of the chart should read 'Metric by Region: [Selection]' (e.g., 'Metric by Region: Revenue').

task_completion.description: Verify that the report has been saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the very top title bar of the Power BI Desktop application window. It should display 'Dynamic_Metric_Report - Power BI Desktop' or show a 'Save As' dialog confirming the filename 'Dynamic_Metric_Report.pbix'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual Power BI Desktop GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the authentic Power BI Desktop application interface (ribbon, canvas, visualization pane, data pane), not a text editor, terminal, or mocked UI.

integrity.description: Verify that the visuals are genuine Power BI components and not mocked text boxes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The slicer and bar chart must be actual interactive visuals with standard Power BI formatting, tooltips, or selection highlights, rather than static shapes or text boxes drawn to look like charts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the DAX formula bar to create measures.
integrity.visual_evidence: At least one screenshot should show the DAX formula bar expanded under the ribbon, displaying the DAX code (e.g., using SUM, SWITCH, or SELECTEDVALUE) for one of the required measures.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The data source sales_data.csv contains columns Sales_Amount, Quantity_Sold, and Region (North, South, East, West). The task requires exactly five DAX measures to be visible in the fields pane: Total_Revenue, Total_Units, Total_Transactions, Selected_Metric_Value, and Chart_Title. It requires a disconnected table named Metric_Param with a Metric column containing values: Revenue, Units, Transactions. The final report must contain a slicer visual and a clustered bar chart visual, and the application window title must reflect the saved file name Dynamic_Metric_Report.pbix.
